title: Daily Leads and Partners
description: For each date and make, count unique leads and unique partners.
constraints: (date_id, make_name, lead_id, partner_id) is the primary key
The same lead or partner can appear multiple times per date/make — use COUNT(DISTINCT)
key_concept: COUNT(DISTINCT col) — count unique values within each group.

hints.L1: GROUP BY date_id and make_name. Use COUNT(DISTINCT lead_id) and COUNT(DISTINCT partner_id).
hints.L2: SELECT date_id, make_name, COUNT(DISTINCT lead_id) AS unique_leads, COUNT(DISTINCT partner_id) AS unique_partners FROM DailySales GROUP BY date_id, make_name
hints.L3: SELECT date_id, make_name, COUNT(DISTINCT lead_id) AS unique_leads, COUNT(DISTINCT partner_id) AS unique_partners FROM DailySales GROUP BY date_id, make_name

optimal_solution: SELECT date_id, make_name, COUNT(DISTINCT lead_id) AS unique_leads, COUNT(DISTINCT partner_id) AS unique_partners FROM DailySales GROUP BY date_id, make_name;
